paragraphs.body: You never expect grief until you live with it. The film Babadook explores this topic throughout the movie. I hadn't seen it until the end of my senior year of high school, during my English class. I learned from viewing that the entire film is about grief and dealing with it. The film is a horror film, but the ending is pleasant, even though the Babadook is only trapped in the basement. The story of the Babadook is a metaphor for grief. I did not realize it at the time, but the meaning of grief from the movie became very real to me only two months later.

paragraphs.body: Grief is an unavoidable part of life, something we all go through at some point. Grief occurs when you may or may not expect it. Whether spilling a hot drink on yourself or being late to a class, having a grievance is a genuine experience. Grief isn't happiness. Grief is a deep, emotional, almost violent shift caused by harm or loss. It messes with the foundation of your life and makes you go through difficult emotions. These moments remind us that life is unpredictable, and it's always changing, no matter how small or significant the moments are. I learned about this reality when I was faced with many grievances.

paragraphs.body: For starters, high school was ending. That might seem like an exciting time for some, but for me, it was full of fear. I was afraid of losing my friends because of the distance and the life I was living. As humans, we fear change, and I was definitely scared. I was on the cusp of turning 18 and becoming an adult. I was going to be living alone, and I was going to be starting college. While my stress levels were high, my stress started to impact the people around me. After Page 1

annotations.text: As humans, we fear change, and I was definitely scared.

paragraphs.body: graduation, summer began, and packing and planning for one of the greatest changes in my life started. Then, one week into July, my uncle passed away from cancer.

annotations.text: Then, one week into July, my uncle passed away from cancer.

paragraphs.body: The grief and stress because of the loss was overwhelming. While few of us accept these changes with time, grief can come from an unexpected part of life that often comes along with grief. My uncle had cancer for many years, and we knew at the end of my senior year that his battle was ending when he entered hospice. Besides my own stressess from college, this development had a large impact on my whole family. Our house became very quiet this summer.

annotations.text: Our house became very quiet this summer.

paragraphs.body: My 18th birthday was 2 weeks after the death of my uncle. I didn't expect much from anyone at this time in our lives, but I selfishly wished for something special. So, I planned a trip with my friends to a cabin in the woods for a few days. Before I went, my mom wanted to have a small family birthday party for me. I chose to just have the family come over and eat Indian food and watch a movie. I didn't expect gifts from my family during this hard time, but my mother handed me a small box. This box was a jewelry box with the most beautiful small earrings inside.

The jewelry box my mother gave me is a little purple box. The box is not much larger than my hand. It is extremely portable and ideal for bringing items to college. Purple is my favorite color, although not standard purple. I prefer light lavender. My mother knew about this, so she tried hard to get a jewelry box in that color. My mother found a great box that matched the color perfectly. It has the same texture as any inexpensive rug and is outlined in gold. Right in the center of the box is the letter Q. I don’t have the most common name, and the first letter is even more uncommon, so the Q in the middle was revolutionary for me. The bottom is arranged in a grid to hold the earrings. It might’ve been a stupid box with silly earrings inside, but they were perfect for me, and I treasure the gift, especially in a time full of grief.

paragraphs.body: Now, it’s been 5 months since the events of the summer, and I have started to understand the movie’s meaning. The Babadook never leaves, but the family learns to live with it. Grief never leaves, but now, 5 months later, my family is learning to live with our grief. I cannot speak for my whole family but for me, the term time heals all wounds has a lot of meaning for me. I look back on the time I had with my uncle, and I smile with happy memories.

annotations.text: The Babadook never leaves, but the family learns to live with it.
annotations.comment: There's definitely a metaphor here :) Let's talk.

paragraphs.body: My uncle has always been a quiet man to me. He always had a chill vibe and was never overbearing. Every year, our small family got together in my grandmother’s even smaller yellow house for any occasion. Whether it was birthdays or holidays, we would always gather there. The house always smelled like my grandmother's. There is no way in words I know to describe the smell of my grandmother's old house except it smelled like her. When I enter my grandmother’s new house, I am reminded of her old house and the memories collected there, specifically of my uncle.

paragraphs.body: Collected on the small couch in the living room, my sister, my cousin, and I sat giggling, the whole house could hear our laughter. While we laugh and share our secret inside jokes, the adults are in the other room sipping their red wine with the background music of funky jazz combined with our obnoxious voices. When the wine was gone, or the conversation got boring, my mother and her brother came to check on us. They joined us on that couch and joined our fun with a song. They would put their arms around us and rock us back and forth while singing “We Are Buddies” on repeat. Then, without warning, they would start violently shaking us while repeating it even faster. Before, we filled the house with our laughter, but when Mom and Uncle Timmy entered the room, our voices shook the house.

annotations.comment: Yes! See? There's more description here. It's wonderful :)

paragraphs.body: Grief and the change in my life have taught me that embracing unavoidable change can create a more positive life for you in the future. Though change is sometimes overwhelming, the Page 3

change caused by grief can make people experience growth. It can make people experience growth. Even if your grief creates a tough reality to accept, it might allow for a more positive and fulfilling life for you in the future if you learn to live with your grief instead of being consumed by it. Grief, though painful, teaches us to value change and the importance of finding strength during a time of hurt. My family made the changes in our life easier to accept as we all struggled together, and we still work together to uplift and protect each other from our grief so we don’t drown in our emotions. The movie The Babadook reminds us not to get swept up in grief and to hold our loved ones close along with the memories of those who are no longer with us.
